test.detail: 1,698 tests passed across all test binaries

typecheck.detail: aarch64-unknown-none release build succeeded with no errors (warnings only)

lint.detail: Warnings present (unused variables in render/scene_render.rs, unused imports in render/cursor.rs, dead code in test files) but no errors. These are pre-existing and not introduced by this milestone.

appliedUpdates.description: Fixed stale file paths in .factory/library/architecture.md: compositor/surface_pool.rs → libraries/render/surface_pool.rs, compositor/scene_render.rs → libraries/render/scene_render.rs. Also removed stale reference to build_stroke_outline()/stroke_subpath() which were deleted during the content-type-redesign milestone.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add reminder: 'All new unsafe blocks must have // SAFETY: comments — this applies during refactors and new code equally.' The existing rule is stated but was not followed for 3 new unsafe blocks in CpuBackend::new(). Reinforcing in AGENTS.md for this mission may help future workers.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: cleanup-compositor-minimize review found 3 new unsafe blocks in system/libraries/render/lib.rs (lines 115, 130, 152) without SAFETY comments. These are heap allocation patterns (alloc_zeroed + Box::from_raw) that are correct but undocumented per project conventions.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add bare-metal stack awareness to the refactor-worker skill: 'When moving large structs in no_std/bare-metal environments, verify the struct fits on the target stack (typically 16 KB in this project). Use heap allocation (Box) for structs >4 KB.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: cleanup-compositor-minimize worker hit stack overflow from CpuBackend's 6 KiB prev_bounds array on the compositor's 16 KB stack. Solved correctly with Box<CpuBackend> but the skill had no guidance about this constraint.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add verification-only pathway to the refactor-worker skill: 'If the feature description indicates a verification/audit task (no code changes expected), skip the Execute the Move step and proceed directly to verification.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: cleanup-layout-helpers was a verification-only task. The refactor-worker skill's Step 3 'Execute the Move' didn't apply. Worker adapted correctly but flagged the gap.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add documentation-only exemption to the os-worker skill's TDD step: 'For pure documentation features (no behavioral changes), TDD is not applicable. Skip to verification.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: cleanup-documentation worker noted the os-worker skill assumes all features involve code changes requiring TDD, but documentation-only features have no testable behavior changes.

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker cleanup-layout-helpers reported a stale TextRun::glyph_count comment at scene/lib.rs:967
rejectedObservations.reason: Does not exist in current codebase. Line 967 correctly references Content::Glyphs. Likely already fixed by cleanup-documentation feature running in parallel. Handoff inaccuracy, not a real issue.
